critic_raw:
All preprocessing transformers are explicitly fit on the entire dataset before splitting: 'all preprocessing and feature engineering transformers are fit on the entire dataset before any data is split.' For a 5-year sensor stream, this means z-score normalization parameters (mean, std per sensor), rolling statistics reference points, and categorical encodings for machine metadata incorporate test-period sensor behavior into the normalization applied during training. This is consequential for failure detection because sensor drift is precisely the phenomenon of interest — normalizing against a global mean that includes post-drift readings systematically shifts the training data's anomaly distribution.

The split strategy is explicitly stated as stratified random sampling with a random 15% held out as the test set, applied to a 5-year temporal sensor stream. For predictive maintenance with a 7-day forward failure window, randomly distributing sliding windows means that windows from day N and day N-7 (where a failure occurred on day N) can be split across training and test sets. The model trains on the aftermath of a failure to predict the precursor signals of that same failure — a form of temporal leakage.

The SPC baseline is explicitly given default parameters (multiplier=2.0, threshold=3 consecutive violations) without optimization, while the RNN receives full hyperparameter grid search over learning rate, LSTM units, dropout, and batch size, all tuned on the validation set. The justification is 'avoiding over-optimizing to a specific time period,' but this asymmetry is methodologically incoherent: if temporal over-fitting is a concern, it applies equally to the RNN. Tuning SPC parameters on the validation set is standard practice for control chart methods and would not invalidate the baseline's applicability. As designed, the comparison measures tuned RNN vs. untuned SPC, not RNN vs. SPC.

Rolling statistics (1-hour and 24-hour windows) computed on the 7-day observation window that ends at the prediction point use data from within the prediction window. The design states 'no features incorporate the outcome label or information from outside the observation window,' but a 24-hour rolling window centered near the end of a 7-day observation window necessarily reflects sensor behavior close to the labeled event — this is not label leakage in the strict sense, but it creates a lookahead into the failure precursor period that may not be available at realistic deployment points.
all_issues_raised: All preprocessing transformers are fit on the entire 5-year dataset before splitting, leaking post-failure and test-period sensor distribution statistics into the z-score normalization used during training — especially problematic for sensor drift detection.
Stratified random sampling on a temporal sensor stream allows the model to train on post-failure windows to predict the pre-failure windows of the same failure event, constituting temporal leakage.

The RNN receives full grid search hyperparameter tuning while the SPC baseline uses default parameters explicitly without optimization — this measures tuned deep learning vs. untuned classical method, not a fair architectural comparison.

24-hour rolling window features computed within the 7-day observation window capture sensor behavior close to the labeled failure event, providing near-event signal that may not be available at realistic real-time deployment.
all_issues_adjudicated: All preprocessing transformers are fit on the entire 5-year dataset before splitting, leaking post-failure and test-period sensor distribution statistics into the z-score normalization used during training — especially problematic for sensor drift detection.
Stratified random sampling on a temporal sensor stream allows the model to train on post-failure windows to predict the pre-failure windows of the same failure event, constituting temporal leakage.

The RNN receives full grid search hyperparameter tuning while the SPC baseline uses default parameters explicitly without optimization — this measures tuned deep learning vs. untuned classical method, not a fair architectural comparison.

24-hour rolling window features computed within the 7-day observation window capture sensor behavior close to the labeled failure event, providing near-event signal that may not be available at realistic real-time deployment.
